name: Buildkite Package Registries
cost: Personal: free (1 GB storage/bandwidth). Pro: $30 USD/active user/month (20 GB included, then $1.25 USD/GB/month, tiered down to $0.25/GB at high volume). Enterprise: custom pricing, 30-user minimum (adds branded endpoints, virtual/composite registries, SLSA provenance, license checks, threat scanning, volume discounts). 30-day All-Access trial, no card required.
language: Ruby (Rails) — inherited from the Packagecloud engine; not officially published, medium confidence
repo: N/A — proprietary, closed source (helper plugins are open at https://github.com/buildkite-plugins; Go SDK at https://github.com/buildkite/go-buildkite)
perf_notes: No published throughput/latency/memory benchmarks (managed SaaS). Composite (virtual) registries never store files and redirect download requests to the upstream's storage, so download performance follows the backing store. Customers can co-locate packages with build/deploy targets via a private storage link (AWS S3 or GCS) to reduce egress latency/bandwidth.
scale_notes: Vendor-operated, highly-available cloud SaaS (Buildkite platform, SOC 2 Type II); HA and scaling are managed by Buildkite, not user-configurable. Descended from Packagecloud, which served package distribution at large scale. Storage scales via Buildkite-hosted storage or customer-owned S3/GCS buckets; usage billed per GB of storage+bandwidth with volume tiers up to 50,000+ GB.
summary: A proprietary, multi-ecosystem SaaS package registry (the rebranded Packagecloud) that hosts private/public PyPI and roughly a dozen other package types with team-based RBAC, OIDC trusted publishing, and optional customer-owned S3/GCS storage.
extras: Composite/virtual registries: merge one or more private source registries plus the ecosystem's official public index as upstreams into a single PyPI URL (Java, JavaScript, Python only); virtual, never store files, redirect to upstream storage

13 ecosystems in one product: Python (PyPI), JavaScript (npm), Java (Maven/Gradle), NuGet, Ruby (RubyGems), OCI (Docker) images, Helm (OCI or Standard) charts, Hugging Face models, Terraform modules, Alpine (apk), Debian (deb), Red Hat (rpm), and generic files
Bring-your-own private storage backend (AWS S3 or Google Cloud Storage) to co-locate packages with deploy targets; defaults to Buildkite-hosted storage
OIDC trusted publishing: registries enforce an OIDC policy on claims (org, pipeline, job ID, branch, commit SHA, agent ID) from buildkite-agent, GitHub Actions, or CircleCI OIDC tokens
SLSA provenance attestations (DSSE envelope, SLSA Build Level 1, Enterprise-only; explicitly NOT PEP 740 format) for all ecosystems except OCI-based ones (Docker, Helm OCI)
Python publishing is via curl HTTP POST or the bk package push CLI (no twine support); install via pip using a PEP 503 /pypi/simple index URL with a basic-auth read token
Tooling: bk package push CLI, Publish-to-Packages and Generate-Provenance Buildkite plugins, REST + GraphQL APIs, go-buildkite SDK, webhooks
Long-lived read-only registry tokens for distribution; per-team Full/Read&Write/Read-only roles plus org-level Security > Packages action controls (Enterprise)
Rebrand of Packagecloud (announced 2024-10-08); existing Packagecloud kept running in parallel
